items.label: Care
items.short_definition: noticing the conditions that allow something to continue

primary_mode.label: Conditions for continuation
primary_mode.text: Your attention may often go toward what needs support, time, maintenance, repair, or the right environment just to keep going.

aspects.label: Sustaining
aspects.attention: You might find yourself observing the repeated, everyday work that keeps things intact.
aspects.strength: This can help you notice the practical effort that allows a situation to keep functioning.
aspects.friction: This often becomes most apparent when a situation relies on effort that is unseen, unsupported, or unevenly distributed.

aspects.label: Cultivating
aspects.attention: Because the {label} orientation leans toward cultivating, you may also notice what is still developing.
aspects.strength: Whether it is a skill, a relationship, or a project, you likely see the conditions that help potential take shape.
aspects.friction: It can stand out when potential exists but the surrounding conditions are rushed, blocked, or misaligned.

aspects.label: Tending
aspects.attention: Additionally, you may pay attention to what still needs tending after a disruption or an ending.
aspects.strength: This can include noticing when a situation requires repair, closure, or simple acknowledgment before it can fully settle.
aspects.friction: The tension appears when something is treated as over while it still needs care, repair, or placement.

items.core_question: What does this situation still need in order to continue, grow, repair, or settle?

items.label: Order
items.short_definition: noticing placement, cause, responsibility, and structure

primary_mode.label: Placement and structure
primary_mode.text: When you look at a situation, your attention may often go toward how things are named, where they belong, what caused them, and what roles they hold.

aspects.label: Cause
aspects.attention: You might frequently notice what actually happened and why, tracking the sequence of actions and the real mechanisms driving an event.
aspects.strength: This awareness can help you look past surface confusion and follow the structure of what set something in motion.
aspects.friction: It can feel frustrating when a situation stays chaotic because its true cause has not been found, or when responsibility is avoided by keeping the root of a problem hidden.

aspects.label: Placement
aspects.attention: Along with tracking causes, you may often notice where things belong and how they fit within a larger structure.
aspects.strength: This can help you recognise roles, boundaries, and arrangements that need to be made clearer.
aspects.friction: This tends to stand out when a role becomes confused or when a boundary is crossed without acknowledgment.

aspects.label: Restoration
aspects.attention: Furthermore, you may focus on what needs to be answered for or set right after a disruption.
aspects.strength: This can help you notice the consequences of a fracture and what it would take to bring a situation back into balance.
aspects.friction: The tension appears when something remains broken, displaced, or unresolved without being recognised or returned to its proper place.

items.core_question: What needs to be clarified, recognized, or returned to its proper place so a situation can finally make sense?
